steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: $f(t) = 51t - 2t^2$ is a downward-opening parabola with $a = -2$, $b = 51$. Its maximum occurs at $t^{\ast} = -\frac{b}{2a} = \frac{51}{4}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Since $g(t) = f(t) + 5$, the maximum of $g$ is $f(t^{\ast}) + 5 = 5 + 2\left(\frac{51}{4}\right)^2$.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — $5+{ \left(\frac{51}{2}\right)}^{2}$: This equals $5 + \frac{51^2}{4}$, which is twice the correct vertex value of $\frac{51^2}{8}$. ✗
  • Choice B — $5+2{ \left(\frac{51}{4}\right)}^{2}$: Equals $5 + \frac{51^2}{8}$, which matches the computed maximum exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — $5-2{ \left(\frac{51}{4}\right)}^{2}$: Subtracts the vertex value instead of adding it; gives a result well below zero. ✗
  • Choice D — $5-{ \left(\frac{51}{2}\right)}^{2}$: Subtracts the wrong (larger) quantity; gives a large negative number. ✗
That is the correct answer.
